mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future earnings and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS changes remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) during rate decline periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0 percent, it signals a structural labor market deterioration that triggers recession risk pricing, causing immediate negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0 percent, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.03) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts confirm broad-based consumer spending strength, which drives corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint positive movement of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.04, or the interaction term loses significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the industrial production index during a late-cycle expansion reflects weakening manufacturing demand, which triggers short-term profit downgrades and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO changes do not correspond to negative 5-day forward returns in late-cycle periods, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below 52 percent in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPIAUCSL index rises above 235.0, it indicates inflation breaching the Federal Reserve target, prompting market expectations of aggressive rate hikes that compress equity valuations and generate negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in over 60 percent of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 235.0, or the threshold signal yields a Rank IC below 0.02 across multiple market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls indicate expanding economic activity and higher aggregate consumption, which directly supports corporate earnings growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no statistically significant correlation with 20-day forward returns (p > 0.05) or produces a Rank IC below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in real GDP and retail sales validates sustained consumer demand momentum, which reduces recession probability and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction between GDPC1 and RSAFS fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.04, or the combined signal underperforms a baseline mean-reversion model.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between falling industrial production and rising unemployment during an economic expansion signals a supply-side shock that erodes profit margins, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns within one month.
failure_condition: The joint signal of declining INDPRO and rising UNRATE during expansions fails to predict negative 20-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.03, or positive returns occur in more than 55 percent of triggered cases.
